title: Prepositions
subtitle: Time, place, and the verbs that insist on a specific partner
intro: Prepositions are the joints of English: a sentence can be grammatically intact and still collapse on one wrong preposition. Examiners treat preposition errors as lexical-grammatical, which means they drag down both LR and GRA bands. The payoff for studying dependent prepositions ('interested in', 'depend on', 'consist of') is enormous because they recur in every task.

rules.heading: Time: at / on / in — zoom levels
rules.body: at a point (at 9pm, at night), on a day (on Monday, on 5 July), in a period (in 2024, in the morning, in the twentieth century).

rules.heading: Place: at / on / in — container logic
rules.body: in = three dimensions (in the room, in London), on = surface (on the wall, on the coast), at = a point or institution (at the station, at university).

rules.heading: Dependent prepositions (verbs)
rules.body: depend on, consist of, result in (cause), result from (effect), respond to, benefit from, focus on, rely on, suffer from, contribute to.

rules.heading: Dependent prepositions (adjectives + nouns)
rules.body: Adjectives: interested in, aware of, capable of, different from, similar to. Nouns: an increase in, a decline in, a solution to, the reason for, an effect on.

examples.sentence: Between 2000 and 2010, there was a sharp increase in renewable-energy investment.
examples.note: 'an increase IN' is fixed; 'Between X and Y' for range.

examples.sentence: Urbanisation has resulted in a marked decline in farmland.
The success of renewable energy depends on consistent government support.
I am particularly interested in how AI will affect creative work.
Children suffering from malnutrition are more vulnerable to infectious disease.

band8_marker: Band 8 writers use noun-noun preposition chains precisely: 'the impact of tourism ON local culture', 'the relationship BETWEEN income AND education'. They also distinguish nuanced pairs: 'worry ABOUT' (general concern) vs. 'worry OVER' (prolonged anxiety), 'agree WITH' (a person/opinion) vs. 'agree ON' (a decision) vs. 'agree TO' (a proposal).

common_errors.fix: 'Discuss' and 'mention' are transitive → 'discuss the topic', 'mention it'.

items.prompt: The outcome depends [A:from] the level of public engagement [B:with] the policy.
There was a notable increase [A:of] unemployment [B:during] the recession.
Many young people suffer [A:from] anxiety [B:about] their future career prospects.
I disagree [A:with] his view [B:on] climate policy.
items.explanation: Both correct: 'disagree with (a person)', 'view on (a topic)'.

items.prompt: The minister refused to discuss [A:about] the scandal [B:with] journalists.
I have been interested [A:with] astronomy [B:since] childhood.
The treatment resulted [A:in] significant improvement [B:in] patients.
Her approach is quite different [A:than] the traditional method [B:of] teaching.
The government should invest more ___ education.
She is responsible ___ quality control at the factory.
This report focuses ___ the long-term effects of automation.
His argument is based ___ outdated data.
There is no easy solution ___ this problem.
Poor air quality has a serious impact ___ respiratory health.
The decline ___ manufacturing jobs began ___ the 1980s.
Governments should protect citizens ___ online fraud.
Unemployment rose sharply ___ the second quarter.
Her essay consisted ___ three main arguments.
There is a clear link ___ sleep deprivation and cognitive decline.
She is an expert ___ renewable energy policy.
The population of the city grew ___ 25% ___ the past decade.
We should not compare apples ___ oranges.
I congratulated her ___ her promotion.
Success in IELTS ultimately depends ___ consistent daily practice.
